crash, resulting in a denial of service.

full_text:
Ubuntu Security Notices

USN-7892-1

 

 

 

 

 

 
USN-7892-1: H2O vulnerability

 

 

 

 
Publication date

 

 

 
26 November 2025

 

 

 

 

 
Overview

 

 

 
H2O could be made to crash if it received specially crafted network traffic.

 

 

 

 

 
Releases

 

 

 

 22.04 LTS

20.04 LTS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Open side navigation
 

 

 
Packages

 

 h2o - the optimized HTTP/1, HTTP/2 server
 

 

 
Details

 

 

It was discovered that H2O exhibited poor server resource management in its
HTTP/2 protocol. An attacker could possibly use this issue to cause H2O to
crash, resulting in a denial of service.

 

 

 

It was discovered that H2O exhibited poor server resource management in its
HTTP/2 protocol. An attacker could possibly use this issue to cause H2O to
crash, resulting in a denial of service.

 

 

 

 
Update instructions

 

 

 

 

In general, a standard system update will make all the necessary changes.

 Learn more about how to get the fixes.
 

 

 

 

 
The problem can be corrected by updating your system to the following package versions:

 

 

 

 

 Ubuntu Release
 Package Version
 

 22.04

LTS jammy

 h2o – 

2.2.5+dfsg2-6.1ubuntu2+esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 libh2o0.13 – 

2.2.5+dfsg2-6.1ubuntu2+esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

 20.04

LTS focal

 h2o – 

2.2.5+dfsg2-3ubuntu0.1~esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 libh2o0.13 – 

2.2.5+dfsg2-3ubuntu0.1~esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

Reduce your security exposure

 

 Ubuntu Pro provides ten-year security coverage to 25,000+ packages in Main and Universe repositories, and it is free for up to five machines.
 

 

 Get Ubuntu Pro
